test_cases.input: Start af-hub for the first time
test_cases.expected: Admin user created; bootstrap token hash stored; plaintext token printed to stdout once; token has 90-day default expiry
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert admin_user_exists_in_db(); assert bootstrap_token_hash_stored(); assert plaintext_not_stored(); assert stdout_contains_token_once(); assert token_expiry == now() + 90_days

test_cases.input: Attempt to run af-hub initialization routine a second time
test_cases.expected: Initialization is not re-run; existing admin user and bootstrap token are not overwritten
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert existing_admin_unchanged(); assert existing_bootstrap_token_unchanged(); assert no_new_admin_created()

test_cases.input: Start af-hub for the first time with BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN_EXPIRY_DAYS=30
test_cases.expected: Bootstrap token is created with expiry of 30 days

preconditions: Admin has a valid admin token
test_cases.input: POST /v1/admin/bootstrap-token/rotate with valid admin bearer token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; new plaintext bootstrap token returned in response body; old token immediately invalidated
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert response.status == 200; assert response.body.contains('new_token'); old_token_request = call_any_endpoint(old_token); assert old_token_request.status == 401; assert old_token_request.body.code == 'token_invalid'

test_cases.input: POST /v1/admin/bootstrap-token/rotate with an expired token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 401 with code token_expired

test_cases.input: POST /v1/admin/bootstrap-token/rotate with a malformed/invalid token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 401 with code token_invalid

preconditions: Non-admin user with valid token exists
test_cases.input: POST /v1/admin/bootstrap-token/rotate with non-admin bearer token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with code permission_denied

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert response.status == 201; assert response.body.username == 'alice'; assert response.body.email == 'alice@example.com'; assert response.body.created_by != null; assert response.body.created_at != null

test_cases.input: PATCH /v1/users/{user_id} with body { username: 'alice_updated' }
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; updated user record returned

test_cases.expected: HTTP 422 with code validation_error

test_cases.input: POST /v1/users with body { username: 'alice', email: 'alice@example.com', oauth_provider: 'github' } (provider_account_id absent)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 422 with code validation_error

test_cases.input: POST /v1/users with body { username: 'alice', email: 'alice@example.com', provider_account_id: 'gh-123' } (oauth_provider absent)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 422 with code validation_error

test_cases.input: POST /v1/users with body { username: 'alice2', email: 'alice@example.com' }
test_cases.expected: HTTP 409 with code conflict

test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with code permission_denied

test_cases.input: PATCH /v1/users/{user_id} with non-admin bearer token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with code permission_denied

preconditions: OAuth provider returns response with verified email

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert user_count_for_email == 1; assert session_token_returned(); assert user.oauth_provider == 'github'

preconditions: User with email 'alice@example.com' already exists (GitHub provider)
OAuth provider returns same email via GitLab
test_cases.input: User completes OAuth flow via GitLab with same email as existing GitHub account
test_cases.expected: No new user record created; new GitLab provider linked to existing account (auto-merge)

preconditions: OAuth provider configured to return response without verified email
test_cases.input: User attempts OAuth flow where provider does not supply a verified email
test_cases.expected: OAuth flow is rejected; appropriate error returned; no account provisioned

preconditions: User runs af-hub signup or login
test_cases.input: CLI calls device authorization endpoint
test_cases.expected: CLI receives device_code, user_code, and verification_uri; prints verification_uri and user_code to terminal

test_cases.input: device_code expiry time passes without user completing browser authorization
test_cases.expected: CLI exits with informative error message indicating authorization was not completed in time; exit code non-zero
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert exit_code != 0; assert stderr_or_stdout.contains('expired') or contains('not completed')

test_cases.input: User runs af-hub signup or login when device authorization endpoint is down
test_cases.expected: CLI displays error and exits non-zero; no token file created or overwritten

test_cases.input: CLI successfully completes signup and stores session token
test_cases.expected: Directory created with 0700 permissions; token file created at ~/.config/af-hub/token with 0600 permissions

test_cases.input: CLI stores session token on Windows
test_cases.expected: Token file created at %APPDATA%\af-hub\token with ACL restricting access to current user only

preconditions: Token file exists at well-known path

test_cases.expected: Token file is deleted; exit code is 0

preconditions: Token file does NOT exist at well-known path
test_cases.input: User runs `af-hub logout`
test_cases.expected: CLI exits successfully with code 0; no error displayed

test_cases.input: CLI command that requires authentication is invoked
test_cases.expected: CLI reads token file and passes session token as Bearer token in Authorization header

test_cases.input: POST /v1/keys with body { expires_at: '<future_datetime>' }
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response contains plaintext base64url key and key_id; only hash stored in DB
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert response.status == 201; assert is_base64url(response.body.key); assert len(decode_base64url(response.body.key)) >= 32; assert db_stores_hash_only(response.body.key_id)

test_cases.input: POST /v1/keys without expires_at in body
test_cases.expected: HTTP 422 with code validation_error

preconditions: Valid API key exists for repo A
test_cases.input: Request to any repo A endpoint using the API key
test_cases.expected: Request authorized with editor role for repo A

preconditions: Key owner has valid bearer token
Key key_id exists and is not expired

test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; key metadata returned with updated expires_at

test_cases.input: PATCH /v1/keys/{key_id} with admin token and body { expires_at: '<new_future_datetime>' }
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; expires_at updated

test_cases.input: PATCH /v1/keys/{key_id} with body { expires_at: '<future>', some_other_field: 'value' }
test_cases.expected: HTTP 422 with code validation_error

preconditions: Key owner has valid bearer token
test_cases.input: PATCH /v1/keys/{key_id} with body { expires_at: '<past_datetime>' }

preconditions: Third-party user token (not key owner, not admin)
test_cases.input: DELETE /v1/keys/{key_id} with non-owner, non-admin token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with code permission_denied

test_cases.input: DELETE /v1/keys/nonexistent-key-id
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with code key_not_found

test_cases.input: Send request to any endpoint with an expired bearer token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 401; Content-Type: application/json; body matches { error: '<string>', code: 'token_expired' }
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert response.status == 401; assert response.headers['Content-Type'] == 'application/json'; assert response.body.code == 'token_expired'; assert 'error' in response.body

test_cases.input: Send request to any endpoint with a malformed bearer token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 401; body matches { error: '<string>', code: 'token_invalid' }

preconditions: Server configured to produce a 500 error on a specific request
test_cases.input: Request that causes an unexpected server-side error
test_cases.expected: HTTP 500; body matches { error: '<string>', code: 'internal_error' }; no internal stack trace in response body
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert response.status == 500; assert response.body.code == 'internal_error'; assert 'stack' not in response.body; assert 'traceback' not in response.body

test_cases.input: Request to a path not matching any defined /v1/ route (e.g. GET /v1/undefined)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404

preconditions: Valid bearer token for repo A
test_cases.input: Verify that user_id and repo_id are derived from the bearer token without query parameters
test_cases.expected: Request scoped to correct repo and user without needing explicit repo_id or user_id query params
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert request.query_params does not contain 'repo_id'; assert response_scoped_to_token_repo()

preconditions: User has valid token for repo A
test_cases.input: Request to access resources in repo B using repo A bearer token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with code repo_not_found

preconditions: User has access to both repo A and repo B with separate tokens
test_cases.input: Use repo A token to call an endpoint; use repo B token to call same endpoint
test_cases.expected: Each token returns only data scoped to its respective repo; no cross-repo leakage
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert response_a.repo_id == 'repo_A'; assert response_b.repo_id == 'repo_B'; assert response_a != response_b

test_cases.input: Trigger each of the defined error conditions (401, 403, 404, 409, 422, 500) across all endpoints
test_cases.expected: Each error response has Content-Type: application/json and body with 'error' (string) and 'code' (canonical vocabulary string)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each error_response: assert is_json(error_response); assert 'error' in error_response.body; assert error_response.body.code in CANONICAL_CODES

property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any call to POST /v1/keys with a valid token and a future expires_at
property_tests.invariant_check: The returned plaintext key decodes from base64url to at least 32 bytes (256 bits); the database contains only a bcrypt/argon2 hash for that key_id; calling POST /v1/keys again returns a different key each time

property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any bearer token or API key whose expires_at has been set to a timestamp in the past
property_tests.invariant_check: For every Phase 1 endpoint, presenting the expired token returns HTTP 401 with code token_expired within the same request cycle

property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any API key or bootstrap token that has been revoked (via DELETE /v1/keys/{key_id} or POST /v1/admin/bootstrap-token/rotate)
property_tests.invariant_check: The very next request presenting the revoked credential to any endpoint returns HTTP 401 with code token_invalid; no grace period is observed

property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any user who authenticates via a second OAuth provider where the returned email matches an existing account
property_tests.invariant_check: After the authentication completes, exactly one user record exists for that email; the new provider is listed among the user's linked providers

property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any record created via POST /v1/users or POST /v1/keys or any other record-creating operation
property_tests.invariant_check: The created record in the database carries non-null repo_id, created_by, and created_at fields

property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any valid API request to af-hub across any endpoint, presented with a bearer token scoped to repo X
property_tests.invariant_check: The resolved user_id and repo_id are derived exclusively from the bearer token; no resource belonging to a different repo is included in the response; no cross-repo data leakage occurs

property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any error condition triggered on any af-hub endpoint (400-class and 500-class)
property_tests.invariant_check: The HTTP response body is valid JSON conforming to { error: string, code: string } and the code value is a member of the canonical Phase 1 vocabulary; Content-Type header is application/json

property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any POSIX system where the CLI successfully completes login or signup
property_tests.invariant_check: The token file at ~/.config/af-hub/token has permissions 0600; the parent directory ~/.config/af-hub/ has permissions 0700; no other user identity on the system can read the file

edge_case_tests.expected: Initialization skipped; no new admin user or bootstrap token created; no data overwritten

preconditions: Bootstrap token rotation has just succeeded
edge_case_tests.input: Old plaintext bootstrap token presented to POST /v1/admin/bootstrap-token/rotate
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 401 with code token_invalid — no grace period

preconditions: User with email 'alice@example.com' and oauth_provider 'github' exists
edge_case_tests.input: POST /v1/users with { username: 'alice2', email: 'alice@example.com', oauth_provider: 'gitlab', provider_account_id: 'gl-456' }
edge_case_tests.expected: No duplicate user created; new GitLab provider linked to existing account

preconditions: OAuth provider is configured to omit verified email from response
edge_case_tests.input: User attempts to complete OAuth flow through provider that returns no verified email
edge_case_tests.expected: OAuth flow rejected; no user account provisioned; error response returned

preconditions: Device authorization endpoint is unreachable (network error)
edge_case_tests.input: User runs af-hub signup or login
edge_case_tests.expected: CLI displays error and exits non-zero; any pre-existing token file is NOT overwritten

edge_case_tests.input: device_code expires before user completes browser authorization
edge_case_tests.expected: CLI exits non-zero with informative message; no token file written
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: assert exit_code != 0; assert 'expired' in cli_output or 'not completed' in cli_output; assert not token_file_created()

preconditions: POSIX filesystem does not support chmod or 0600 permission setting
edge_case_tests.input: CLI attempts to store token file on a filesystem without POSIX permission support
edge_case_tests.expected: CLI displays error and exits non-zero; token file with insecure permissions is NOT created
CLI exits with code 0; no error displayed

preconditions: Key has just been successfully deleted via DELETE /v1/keys/{key_id}
edge_case_tests.input: Present the revoked plaintext API key to any endpoint
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 401 with code token_invalid immediately — no grace period

edge_case_tests.input: PATCH /v1/keys/{key_id} with body { expires_at: '<timestamp 1 second in the past>' }
Trigger any error condition and inspect the code field in the response
edge_case_tests.expected: code value is always one of: token_expired, token_invalid, permission_denied, user_not_found, key_not_found, repo_not_found, conflict, validation_error, internal_error — no other codes

preconditions: User has tokens for both repo A and repo B
edge_case_tests.input: Use repo A token to access a resource that belongs to repo B
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 404 with code repo_not_found; no data from repo B is leaked
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: assert response.status == 404; assert response.body.code == 'repo_not_found'; assert 'repo_B_data' not in response.body

edge_case_tests.input: Attempt to retrieve the new plaintext bootstrap token again via any endpoint
edge_case_tests.expected: Plaintext token is not available; no endpoint returns it after the initial rotation response

smoke_tests.trigger: Run af-hub for the first time (EP-001)
expected_effects: Admin user record is created in the database
Bootstrap token hash is stored (bcrypt or argon2) — plaintext is NOT stored
Plaintext bootstrap token is printed to stdout exactly once
Bootstrap token has an expiry set (default 90 days from initialization)

smoke_tests.trigger: New external user runs `af-hub signup` CLI command (EP-002)
expected_effects: CLI calls device authorization endpoint and receives device_code, user_code, verification_uri
CLI prints verification_uri and user_code to terminal
CLI polls token endpoint until authorization is granted
New user account is provisioned in the database
Session token is returned to CLI
Token file is written to ~/.config/af-hub/token (Linux/macOS) with 0600 permissions
Parent directory created with 0700 permissions if it did not exist

smoke_tests.trigger: Existing user runs `af-hub login` CLI command (EP-003)
expected_effects: CLI completes device authorization grant flow
Existing user session is resumed — no duplicate account is created
Session token is returned and token file is overwritten with correct permissions

smoke_tests.trigger: Authenticated user calls POST /v1/keys with valid bearer token and a future expires_at (EP-004)
expected_effects: HTTP 201 is returned
Response body contains plaintext base64url-encoded key and key_id
Only the bcrypt/argon2 hash is stored in the database
Key record carries expires_at, key_id, repo_id, created_by, created_at

smoke_tests.trigger: Key owner calls DELETE /v1/keys/{key_id} with valid bearer token (EP-005)
expected_effects: HTTP 200 is returned confirming revocation
Key is immediately marked revoked in the database
Subsequent request with the revoked key returns HTTP 401 with code token_invalid

smoke_tests.trigger: Admin calls POST /v1/admin/bootstrap-token/rotate with valid admin token (EP-006)
expected_effects: HTTP 200 is returned with new plaintext bootstrap token in response body
Old bootstrap token is immediately invalidated
Only the hash of the new token is stored
Old plaintext token presented to any endpoint returns HTTP 401
Token file is deleted if it exists
CLI exits with code 0 regardless of whether the file existed

gaps: REQ-012 (RBAC extensibility) and REQ-013 (TLS enforcement) have no automated functional test cases — these are architectural/operational requirements verified by design review and infrastructure configuration audit rather than in-process unit or integration tests.
REQ-013-AC-01 (TLS in production) requires an environment-level verification (e.g. SSL certificate check against a public endpoint) not covered by unit or component tests.
Performance characteristics of bcrypt/argon2 hashing under load are not covered — deferred to a future phase per the PRD non-functional requirements.
Windows-specific token file ACL test (TC-025) may require a Windows CI runner; coverage depends on CI environment availability.
OAuth provider-side behaviour (e.g. provider returning malformed email, provider downtime) is partially mockable but full end-to-end coverage requires live OAuth provider integration tests.
